title: POST /api/weight — log new weight entry
description: Implement the endpoint to log a new weight entry for the current user. The date is always set to today server-side — the client does not provide it.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/weight with valid weight_value returns 201 with the created entry
recorded_at is set to today's date server-side (client cannot override it)
weight_value must be a positive number — negative or zero returns 422
Entry is associated with the current authenticated user
Returns 401 if no valid JWT provided

steps.details: In src/api/schemas/weight.py, add CreateWeightRequest(BaseModel) with: weight_value (float, gt=0). The gt=0 constraint ensures only positive values are accepted.

In src/api/routers/weight.py, add POST / route. Handler: accept CreateWeightRequest body, create WeightEntry(id=str(uuid4()), user_id=current_user.id, weight_value=request.weight_value, recorded_at=date.today()), add to db and commit, refresh the entry, return WeightEntryResponse with status 201.

POST to /api/weight with {"weight_value": 185.5} and valid JWT. Verify: status 201, response includes id, weight_value=185.5, recorded_at=today's date. POST with weight_value=0 — verify 422. POST with weight_value=-5 — verify 422. POST without token — verify 401.

technicalNotes: Use date.today() from Python's datetime module — this will use the server's local date
Do not accept recorded_at from the client — it must always be server-side today
